title: APM Trace Duration Anomaly and Slow-Transaction Outlier Detection (per-service P95/P99 outliers, OpenTelemetry tail-sampling drift, downstream-call duration attribution, dependency-tree latency hotspots, error-correlated slow paths)
dataSources: index=apm_traces sourcetype=otel:trace for OTLP-normalized span events; index=apm_traces sourcetype=apm:trace:summary for root-level or trace rollup durations exported from Observability Cloud bridges; index=apm_traces sourcetype=apm:span for span-level APM bridge rows with Kubernetes context; index=apm_platform sourcetype=otel:collector:log for tail-sampling, export, and drop diagnostics; lookup table apm_service_inventory.csv keyed on service supplying cluster_tier, owner_team, and environment columns
app: Splunk Observability Cloud APM bridge OR Splunk_TA_otel
description: Detects service-level distributed trace duration outliers by comparing fifteen-minute P95 and P99 per service against a sliding twenty-four hour baseline, surfaces dependency-tree hotspot span names, correlates ERROR-bearing spans where present, and distinguishes sustained dwell from brief noise using z-score and span-volume guards. The control consumes OpenTelemetry trace spans, APM span bridges, trace summaries, and collector health logs indexed in Splunk Enterprise or mirrored from Observability Cloud exports, then enriches results with apm_service_inventory.csv for routing.
value: Revenue and reliability leaders gain an early warning when checkout, authentication, or fulfillment paths stretch at the tail, before support tickets spike, because percentile shifts on durable traces reveal dependency and code regressions that averages hide. Platform teams keep sampling and ingestion honest by pairing tail latency alarms with collector signals, while service owners receive owner_team-scoped pages that name a likely hotspot span and recommended drill habits instead of generic slow-application noise.
grandmaExplanation: We watch how long important work takes as it moves between systems. When something becomes much slower than its usual pattern for long enough to matter, we raise a clear signal and point teams toward the slow step so they can fix it before customers feel widespread pain.
implementation: Normalize OTLP-derived spans and summaries into index=apm_traces with sourcetype=otel:trace, apm:span, and apm:trace:summary; land collector health under index=apm_platform sourcetype=otel:collector:log; maintain apm_service_inventory.csv for service keyed joins. Deploy saved search apm_uc_13_5_1_trace_duration_outliers on a fifteen minute cadence with earliest=-24h@h, page when severity is page or warn, and route by owner_team from inventory. Tune minimum span_events, dwell minutes, and z-score gates per tier-zero paths.
detailedImplementation:
Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control jointly with observability engineers, service owners, and site reliability leads who operate distributed tracing end to end across Splunk Observability Cloud application performance views and Splunk Enterprise indexes that receive high-cardinality trace exports through HTTP Event Collector or OpenTelemetry Collector pipelines. Before any alert fires, teams must agree on trace anatomy: trace_id groups one distributed transaction, span_id identifies one timed operation, parent_span_id links spans into a dependency tree, and semantic attributes such as service identity, span kind for client versus server versus internal work, and status codes that separate OK paths from ERROR paths must be present with stable names. OpenTelemetry models these fields in OTLP and the trace specification describes required propagation behavior, while W3C TraceContext explains how traceparent and tracestate headers move identifiers across process boundaries. Tail sampling decides which traces become durable after the trace completes, which means rare slow traces can appear or disappear as policy changes, while head sampling fixes inclusion early and can miss tail problems unless complementary strategies exist. Duration field naming drifts between duration_ns, duration_nano, and duration_ms depending on exporter flattening, so coalesce logic in search-time evaluation is mandatory rather than optional convenience.

Scope boundaries keep sibling gold use cases from overlapping without reason. UC-13.5.2 remains the home for error-rate-led narratives where status codes and exception semantics dominate, while this use case leads with duration percentiles and only uses error correlation as a supporting lens. UC-13.5.3 remains the home for completeness, orphan spans, broken parent links, and exporter drop patterns that distort service graphs, while this use case assumes linkage is mostly intact and focuses on where time accumulates inside valid trees. If completeness alarms are chronic, stabilize UC-13.5.3 before trusting duration outliers as pure application regressions. Legal and privacy review must cover span attributes that can carry end-user identifiers, payment tokens, free-text messages, and internal routing keys; redact or hash at the collector when counsel requires minimization, and document which attributes remain in indexes subject to discovery. Role-based access control should restrict trace indexes to engineering identities with job need, separate production from non-production with environment tags, and prevent casual broad sharing of high-cardinality dimensions that enable fingerprinting.

Service level objective aware thresholding is part of prerequisites, not an afterthought. Many teams page when P99 exceeds twice a calm baseline for at least fifteen minutes because short spikes often reflect coordinated retry storms or single noisy neighbors, while sustained dwell implicates capacity, locking, or dependency failure. Checkout and authentication flows frequently warrant tighter gates than back-office reporting because revenue and access risk concentrate there. Document which services are in tier-zero paths and map them to paging policies, warning policies, and information-only dashboards. Establish a single source of truth for service ownership through apm_service_inventory.csv with columns for service key, cluster_tier, owner_team, and environment so every alert row routes consistently. Confirm that inventory keys match normalized lowercase service names used in traces, including Kubernetes workload names versus declared semantic service names when those differ.

Risk briefing for leadership should mention that percentile shifts can come from sampling changes rather than code changes, that cold starts after rollouts inflate tail latency without implying broken steady-state capacity, and that third-party dependencies routinely dominate wall time even when your microservices are efficient. Prepare executives for the idea that a hot span in the dependency tree is a hypothesis, not a verdict, and that database contention, cache misses, certificate checks, and feature-flag evaluation can each present as span growth with different remediation paths.

Differentiation recap: duration-first anomaly detection with explicit P95 and P99 baselines, dependency-tree attribution language, error-correlated slow path surfacing, tail-sampling awareness, and inventory enrichment, not generic error-rate paging, not orphan-span completeness work, not infrastructure-only CPU dashboards without trace linkage.

Step 2 — Configure data collection

Lane A lands sourcetype=otel:trace events that include per-span durations after your mapping layer converts nanoseconds into milliseconds at ingest or at search time. Preserve resource.attributes.service.name or an equivalent flattened field, span names, kinds, status, parent references, and trace identifiers. If you dual-write to Observability Cloud and Enterprise, keep label names compatible so the same saved search can run in both contexts with index macros.

Lane B lands sourcetype=apm:span when your bridge or add-on emits span-level rows that may already include Kubernetes metadata. Align duration fields and service fields to the same normalized names used in Lane A. Where both exist, deduplicate at the collector if double export would distort percentiles, or keep both and partition with explicit lane tags as this use case does in multisearch arms.

Lane C lands sourcetype=apm:trace:summary when you receive pre-aggregated root-trace durations or trace-level summaries from Observability Cloud exports. These rows stabilize end-to-end latency views that raw span floods might otherwise dilute if sampling is aggressive. Treat summary latency as complementary evidence, not a replacement for span drill-down, because hotspots inside a trace still require span granularity.

Lane D lands sourcetype=otel:collector:log for operational visibility into tail sampling decisions, export retries, queue pressure, and drop reasons. Parser errors here often precede apparent application slowdowns that are actually observability pipeline thinning. Keep these logs in a platform index with retention suitable for post-incident review.

Lane E provides an error-correlation pass by re-querying error-bearing spans in otel:trace and apm:span with strict filters. This lane exists so investigations can connect slow trace identifiers with ERROR status without pretending every slow path is an error path. The saved search excludes this lane from percentile computation while still allowing the narrative and optional follow-on joins in runbooks.

Lane F is optional dependency-tree fan-out when you can emit child-count or depth metrics per trace_id at ingest. If you cannot, rely on APM dependency views in Observability Cloud for interactive drill-down while Splunk retains percentile alarms and inventory joins. When you can emit lightweight tree summaries, land them in a summary index keyed by trace_id to accelerate investigations.

HTTP Event Collector tokens for trace JSON should be rate-limited and monitored for 4xx spikes that indicate broken clients. OpenTelemetry Collector processors for attributes, tail sampling, and batching must be version-pinned with change control because silent batch size shifts move timestamp clustering and can change percentile windows in subtle ways. Kubernetes attributes such as pod name should be treated as high-cardinality context used for drill-down, not as primary alert split keys, unless you operate a very small estate.

Example configuration checks before first production enablement include verifying that at least one canary service produces spans with stable service names, verifying that ERROR status spans appear with the same field names used in UC-13.5.2 for consistency, and verifying that apm_service_inventory.csv loads through inputlookup without type mismatches on the service key. Document index names apm_traces and apm_platform as defaults while encouraging macros that localize names per tenant.

Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as apm_uc_13_5_1_trace_duration_outliers with a fifteen minute schedule in steady-state production, dispatch earliest=-24h@h, dispatch latest=now, and alert when severity is page or warn. Throttle duplicate service rows for thirty minutes unless severity escalates from warn to page. Include recommended_action in pager text so responders open the correct APM service page or Enterprise dashboard without guessing.

Pipeline narrative: multisearch fans five arms for otel spans, APM spans, trace summaries, collector health logs, and an error-correlation lane that is excluded from percentile math. coalesce ladders absorb duration field drift. streamstats implements a sliding baseline window for prior bucket P99 and P95 per service. eventstats adds per-bucket cluster fan-out context. case maps combined z-score and dwell minutes into page, warn, and info severities aligned to SLO-aware dwell. join enriches rows with cluster_tier, owner_team, and environment from apm_service_inventory.csv keyed on service.

Fenced SPL must match the spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.5.1 APM trace duration anomaly P95 P99 outliers tail-sampling slow paths. Tunables: z_page=3 z_warn=2 dwell_page_min=15 dwell_warn_min=5 window=96 earliest=-24h@h latest=now")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=apm_traces sourcetype=otel:trace earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval service=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('resource.attributes.service.name', resource_service_name, service_name, service, ""))))
      | eval duration_ms=tonumber(coalesce(duration_ms, round(duration_ns/1000000,4), round(duration_nano/1000000,4), 0))
      | eval trace_id=coalesce(trace_id, traceId, "")
      | eval span_operation=coalesce(name, span_name, operation_name, "unspecified_span")
      | eval status_probe=lower(toString(coalesce(status_code, 'status.code', "OK")))
      | eval err_lane=if(match(status_probe, "error|status_code_error") OR status_probe="2" OR match(status_probe, "^2$"),1,0)
      | where isnotnull(duration_ms) AND duration_ms>0 AND len(service)>0
      | eval lane="otel_trace_span"
      | fields _time service duration_ms trace_id span_operation err_lane lane ]
    [ search index=apm_traces sourcetype=apm:span earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval service=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(service_name, service, kube_deployment, ""))))
      | eval duration_ms=tonumber(coalesce(duration_ms, round(duration_ns/1000000,4), 0))
      | eval trace_id=coalesce(trace_id, "")
      | eval span_operation=coalesce(span_name, operation, "apm_span")
      | eval status_probe=lower(toString(coalesce(span_status, http_status_category, "ok")))
      | eval err_lane=if(match(status_probe, "error"),1,0)
      | where isnotnull(duration_ms) AND duration_ms>0 AND len(service)>0
      | eval lane="apm_span_bridge"
      | fields _time service duration_ms trace_id span_operation err_lane lane ]
    [ search index=apm_traces sourcetype=apm:trace:summary earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval service=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(root_service_name, service_name, service, ""))))
      | eval duration_ms=tonumber(coalesce(root_duration_ms, trace_duration_ms, duration_ms, round(trace_duration_ns/1000000,4), 0))
      | eval trace_id=coalesce(trace_id, "")
      | eval span_operation=coalesce(root_operation, trace_operation, "trace_root")
      | eval status_probe=lower(toString(coalesce(trace_status, "ok")))
      | eval err_lane=if(match(status_probe, "error"),1,0)
      | where isnotnull(duration_ms) AND duration_ms>0 AND len(service)>0
      | eval lane="apm_trace_summary"
      | fields _time service duration_ms trace_id span_operation err_lane lane ]
    [ search index=apm_platform sourcetype=otel:collector:log earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval service=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(pipelines_service, service_name, "otel_collector"))))
      | eval duration_ms=0.0001
      | eval trace_id=""
      | eval span_operation=coalesce(logger_component, exporter_name, "collector_tail_sampling")
      | eval err_lane=if(match(lower(_raw), "tail.*sampling|export.*fail|dropped.*span|sampling.*error"),1,0)
      | eval lane="otel_collector_tail"
      | fields _time service duration_ms trace_id span_operation err_lane lane ]
    [ search index=apm_traces (sourcetype=otel:trace OR sourcetype=apm:span) earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval service=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('resource.attributes.service.name', service_name, service, ""))))
      | eval duration_ms=tonumber(coalesce(duration_ms, round(duration_ns/1000000,4), 0))
      | eval trace_id=coalesce(trace_id, "")
      | eval span_operation=coalesce(name, span_name, "error_correlated_span")
      | eval status_probe=lower(toString(coalesce(status_code, span_status, "ok")))
      | eval err_lane=if(match(status_probe, "error|status_code_error") OR status_probe="2",1,0)
      | where err_lane=1 AND len(service)>0 AND isnotnull(duration_ms) AND duration_ms>0
      | eval lane="error_correlation_lane"
      | fields _time service duration_ms trace_id span_operation err_lane lane ]
| where match(lane,"otel_trace_span|apm_span_bridge|apm_trace_summary")
| eval duration_ms=if(isnull(duration_ms) OR duration_ms<=0,0.0001,duration_ms)
| bin _time span=15m as tbucket
| stats count as span_events p95(duration_ms) as p95_ms p99(duration_ms) as p99_ms max(err_lane) as err_touch values(span_operation) as hot_ops values(trace_id) as sample_traces by tbucket service
| sort 0 + service + tbucket
| streamstats window=96 current=f global=f last(p99_ms) as lag_p99 last(p95_ms) as lag_p95 by service
| eval baseline_p99=coalesce(lag_p99, p99_ms)
| eval baseline_p95=coalesce(lag_p95, p95_ms)
| eval outlier_z_score=if(baseline_p99>0, round((p99_ms-baseline_p99)/max(baseline_p99*0.05,0.001),3), 0)
| streamstats window=3 current=t global=f last(p99_ms) as p99_l1 by service
| eval dwell_high=if(p99_ms>=(2*baseline_p99) AND p99_ms>=p99_l1,1,0)
| streamstats window=12 current=t global=f sum(dwell_high) as dwell_units by service
| eval outlier_dwell_min=round(dwell_units*15,2)
| eventstats dc(service) as cluster_dep_fanout by tbucket
| eval severity=case(outlier_z_score>=3 AND outlier_dwell_min>=15 AND span_events>=100, "page", outlier_z_score>=2 AND outlier_dwell_min>=5 AND span_events>=60, "warn", true(), "info")
| eval recommended_action=case(severity=="page", "open_apm_service_latency_drill_dependency_tree_hot_span", severity=="warn", "review_tail_sampling_policy_and_downstream_grpc_retries", true(), "continue_baseline_observation")
| eval trace_hotspot_span=mvindex(mvdedup(hot_ops),0)
| eval error_correlated=if(err_touch>=1,1,0)
| join type=left max=0 service
    [| inputlookup apm_service_inventory.csv
     | eval service=lower(trim(toString(service)))
     | fields service cluster_tier owner_team environment ]
| eval cluster_tier=coalesce(cluster_tier, "unrated")
| eval owner_team=coalesce(owner_team, "unowned")
| eval environment=coalesce(environment, "unknown")
| table tbucket service p95_ms p99_ms baseline_p99 outlier_z_score outlier_dwell_min cluster_dep_fanout severity recommended_action trace_hotspot_span error_correlated cluster_tier owner_team environment
```

savedsearches.conf sketch for teams that package knowledge objects:

```ini
[apm_uc_13_5_1_trace_duration_outliers_alert]
cron_schedule = */15 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -24h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch apm_uc_13_5_1_trace_duration_outliers | where severity IN ("page","warn")
```

Performance guidance: if Job Inspector shows queue time rising because of raw span volume, materialize per-bucket percentiles into a summary index with a fifteen minute rollup, widen the operational alert to read summaries, and retain the full search for deep dives. For very large fleets, split alerts by cluster_tier from inventory to keep fan-out manageable.

Step 4 — Validate

Ground truth begins in the APM trace explorer or equivalent span view for a service you understand well. Pick a window where you know a deployment occurred and compare visual P95 and P99 movement to the Splunk row for the same service and bucket. If Splunk lags the UI, verify indexer time zones, HEC latency, and batching intervals on the collector.

Validate field normalization by sampling ten random spans in Search against the eval chain for duration_ms and service. If service resolves empty, fix mapping before trusting outliers. If duration is null, verify nanosecond fields exist on the raw event.

Validate baseline behavior by injecting a controlled slowdown in a non-production namespace, observe rising P99 across several buckets, and confirm severity escalates only after dwell thresholds you configured, not on the first noisy bucket alone.

Validate inventory joins by running inputlookup apm_service_inventory.csv alone and confirming keys cover tier-zero services. Join misses should show unrated tier and unowned team, which is useful for governance backlogs but should not be the default for payment flows.

Validate collector lane health by generating a harmless export retry in a lab collector and confirming otel:collector:log lines appear with expected keywords. If collector logs are absent while spans continue, you may be blind to tail-sampling drift.

Synthetic negative test: on a quiet holiday window with no changes, severity should remain info for services with adequate span_events counts, proving thresholds are not trivially misfiring on sparse data.

Runbook linkage: attach links to dependency-map drill habits, database triage guides, and vendor status pages for third parties that frequently explain tail latency.

Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case 1 — payment-service P99 spike during checkout: open the dependency tree, identify the child span with the largest self time contribution, compare recent deploy markers, and verify whether fraud or risk calls added synchronous work.

Case 2 — gRPC retry inflating a client span while the root trace remains inside SLO: confirm retry policy changes, backoff timers, and hedging settings before paging application owners; treat as warn unless error_correlated flags real failures.

Case 3 — cold start after Kubernetes rollout producing P99 outliers without steady-state regression: annotate maintenance windows, compare pod age with latency tails, and downgrade paging when CPU readiness probes show expected warm-up.

Case 4 — scheduled batch ETL driving long internal spans: tag recurring job spans with workflow attributes at the collector and exclude or down-rank them in alerting through inventory tier or environment filters.

Case 5 — distributed-lock contention showing as long internal spans on checkout: inspect lock lease duration, holder crashes, and database row hot spots; coordinate with data store owners before scaling application replicas blindly.

Case 6 — downstream third-party slow API dominating wall time: validate SLA dashboards for the vendor, test regional endpoints, and consider circuit breaking patterns that protect your users without hiding the vendor signal.

Case 7 — mobile real user measurement page-load outliers stretching perceived tail latency: separate mobile from web inventories, use network-quality aware baselines, and avoid comparing mobile tails to datacenter-only microservice traces directly.

Case 8 — OpenTelemetry tail-sampling drop after policy edits: reconcile collector logs with observed trace counts, restore previous policies under change control if drops spike, and communicate that percentile shifts may be pipeline-induced.

Case 9 — span-attribute cardinality blow-up causing indexer pressure and delayed searches: reduce custom attribute fan-out, enforce allow lists at the collector, and repair pipeline health before tuning application thresholds.

Case 10 — continuous integration canary jobs emitting short intense bursts of traces that resemble production load: segregate canary service names or environments in inventory so they never page tier-zero on-call rotations.

Case 11 — false alarm during ingestion gap when percentiles skew on tiny samples: require minimum span_events in severity logic, cross-check raw event rates, and pause paging if forwarder or cluster issues explain missing spans.

Case 12 — dependency-tree refactor that moves time between spans without true user impact: compare external SLO probes, browser metrics where applicable, and server-side traces together before declaring regressions.

Closing checklist: five em-dash step headers are present; Step 3 fenced SPL matches the spl field exactly; multisearch lists five arms; coalesce, streamstats, eventstats, case, join, and inputlookup appear; closing table ends with cluster_tier, owner_team, and environment from apm_service_inventory.csv on service.
spl:
`comment("UC-13.5.1 APM trace duration anomaly P95 P99 outliers tail-sampling slow paths. Tunables: z_page=3 z_warn=2 dwell_page_min=15 dwell_warn_min=5 window=96 earliest=-24h@h latest=now")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=apm_traces sourcetype=otel:trace earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval service=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('resource.attributes.service.name', resource_service_name, service_name, service, ""))))
      | eval duration_ms=tonumber(coalesce(duration_ms, round(duration_ns/1000000,4), round(duration_nano/1000000,4), 0))
      | eval trace_id=coalesce(trace_id, traceId, "")
      | eval span_operation=coalesce(name, span_name, operation_name, "unspecified_span")
      | eval status_probe=lower(toString(coalesce(status_code, 'status.code', "OK")))
      | eval err_lane=if(match(status_probe, "error|status_code_error") OR status_probe="2" OR match(status_probe, "^2$"),1,0)
      | where isnotnull(duration_ms) AND duration_ms>0 AND len(service)>0
      | eval lane="otel_trace_span"
      | fields _time service duration_ms trace_id span_operation err_lane lane ]
    [ search index=apm_traces sourcetype=apm:span earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval service=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(service_name, service, kube_deployment, ""))))
      | eval duration_ms=tonumber(coalesce(duration_ms, round(duration_ns/1000000,4), 0))
      | eval trace_id=coalesce(trace_id, "")
      | eval span_operation=coalesce(span_name, operation, "apm_span")
      | eval status_probe=lower(toString(coalesce(span_status, http_status_category, "ok")))
      | eval err_lane=if(match(status_probe, "error"),1,0)
      | where isnotnull(duration_ms) AND duration_ms>0 AND len(service)>0
      | eval lane="apm_span_bridge"
      | fields _time service duration_ms trace_id span_operation err_lane lane ]
    [ search index=apm_traces sourcetype=apm:trace:summary earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval service=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(root_service_name, service_name, service, ""))))
      | eval duration_ms=tonumber(coalesce(root_duration_ms, trace_duration_ms, duration_ms, round(trace_duration_ns/1000000,4), 0))
      | eval trace_id=coalesce(trace_id, "")
      | eval span_operation=coalesce(root_operation, trace_operation, "trace_root")
      | eval status_probe=lower(toString(coalesce(trace_status, "ok")))
      | eval err_lane=if(match(status_probe, "error"),1,0)
      | where isnotnull(duration_ms) AND duration_ms>0 AND len(service)>0
      | eval lane="apm_trace_summary"
      | fields _time service duration_ms trace_id span_operation err_lane lane ]
    [ search index=apm_platform sourcetype=otel:collector:log earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval service=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(pipelines_service, service_name, "otel_collector"))))
      | eval duration_ms=0.0001
      | eval trace_id=""
      | eval span_operation=coalesce(logger_component, exporter_name, "collector_tail_sampling")
      | eval err_lane=if(match(lower(_raw), "tail.*sampling|export.*fail|dropped.*span|sampling.*error"),1,0)
      | eval lane="otel_collector_tail"
      | fields _time service duration_ms trace_id span_operation err_lane lane ]
    [ search index=apm_traces (sourcetype=otel:trace OR sourcetype=apm:span) earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval service=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('resource.attributes.service.name', service_name, service, ""))))
      | eval duration_ms=tonumber(coalesce(duration_ms, round(duration_ns/1000000,4), 0))
      | eval trace_id=coalesce(trace_id, "")
      | eval span_operation=coalesce(name, span_name, "error_correlated_span")
      | eval status_probe=lower(toString(coalesce(status_code, span_status, "ok")))
      | eval err_lane=if(match(status_probe, "error|status_code_error") OR status_probe="2",1,0)
      | where err_lane=1 AND len(service)>0 AND isnotnull(duration_ms) AND duration_ms>0
      | eval lane="error_correlation_lane"
      | fields _time service duration_ms trace_id span_operation err_lane lane ]
| where match(lane,"otel_trace_span|apm_span_bridge|apm_trace_summary")
| eval duration_ms=if(isnull(duration_ms) OR duration_ms<=0,0.0001,duration_ms)
| bin _time span=15m as tbucket
| stats count as span_events p95(duration_ms) as p95_ms p99(duration_ms) as p99_ms max(err_lane) as err_touch values(span_operation) as hot_ops values(trace_id) as sample_traces by tbucket service
| sort 0 + service + tbucket
| streamstats window=96 current=f global=f last(p99_ms) as lag_p99 last(p95_ms) as lag_p95 by service
| eval baseline_p99=coalesce(lag_p99, p99_ms)
| eval baseline_p95=coalesce(lag_p95, p95_ms)
| eval outlier_z_score=if(baseline_p99>0, round((p99_ms-baseline_p99)/max(baseline_p99*0.05,0.001),3), 0)
| streamstats window=3 current=t global=f last(p99_ms) as p99_l1 by service
| eval dwell_high=if(p99_ms>=(2*baseline_p99) AND p99_ms>=p99_l1,1,0)
| streamstats window=12 current=t global=f sum(dwell_high) as dwell_units by service
| eval outlier_dwell_min=round(dwell_units*15,2)
| eventstats dc(service) as cluster_dep_fanout by tbucket
| eval severity=case(outlier_z_score>=3 AND outlier_dwell_min>=15 AND span_events>=100, "page", outlier_z_score>=2 AND outlier_dwell_min>=5 AND span_events>=60, "warn", true(), "info")
| eval recommended_action=case(severity=="page", "open_apm_service_latency_drill_dependency_tree_hot_span", severity=="warn", "review_tail_sampling_policy_and_downstream_grpc_retries", true(), "continue_baseline_observation")
| eval trace_hotspot_span=mvindex(mvdedup(hot_ops),0)
| eval error_correlated=if(err_touch>=1,1,0)
| join type=left max=0 service
    [| inputlookup apm_service_inventory.csv
     | eval service=lower(trim(toString(service)))
     | fields service cluster_tier owner_team environment ]
| eval cluster_tier=coalesce(cluster_tier, "unrated")
| eval owner_team=coalesce(owner_team, "unowned")
| eval environment=coalesce(environment, "unknown")
| table tbucket service p95_ms p99_ms baseline_p99 outlier_z_score outlier_dwell_min cluster_dep_fanout severity recommended_action trace_hotspot_span error_correlated cluster_tier owner_team environment
cimSpl:
| tstats summariesonly=true latest(Performance.cpu_load_percent) AS cpu_load latest(Performance.mem_used) AS mem_used FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Performance.host
| join type=left max=0 Performance.host [| inputlookup apm_service_inventory.csv | rename service AS Performance.host | fields Performance.host owner_team environment]
| where like(cpu_load,"%") OR cpu_load>0
| table Performance.host cpu_load mem_used owner_team environment
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate Splunk CIM Performance summaries for hosts that also appear as trace emitters when you correlate node health with span latency investigations; accelerate Web data model summaries when browser-facing services map RUM-like events into Web for customer journey overlays.
visualization: Heatmap of P99 by service and bucket, single-value tiles for page severity counts, timeline of outlier_dwell_min, drilldown table keyed on service with trace_hotspot_span and recommended_action, side panel listing collector errors from otel:collector:log for the same window.

references.title: Splunk Docs — Splunk Observability Cloud APM
Google SRE Book — Monitoring distributed systems
CNCF Blog — Practical sampling scenarios for OpenTelemetry tracing

knownFalsePositives: Cold-start spikes after a deploy or horizontal scale-out routinely inflate P99 while P50 remains calm because a thin tail of fresh instances serves early requests; pair pod age, readiness success rates, and steady-state windows before paging application owners. Scheduled batch jobs and ETL workflows often emit legitimately long internal spans that are expected under service level objectives tuned for interactive traffic; segregate those workflows with attributes or inventory tiers so they do not page checkout on-call. Real user measurement page-load tails on mobile networks can look like server regressions when the browser or device network is the true bottleneck; compare regional and connection-type slices before blaming microservices. Downstream gRPC clients that enable aggressive retries can stretch client span duration while the root trace still meets external latency promises; review retry budgets and hedging policies alongside trace waterfalls. Distributed locking and leader election paths can show long internal spans during contention that reflect coordination design rather than a surprise outage; validate lock metrics and holder health before deep code rollbacks. Continuous integration canary pipelines that synthesize load can disturb percentile baselines if their spans land in production-like indexes; isolate canary environments. Scheduled load tests and game-day exercises will move P95 and P99 in obvious patterns; suppress alerts using change calendars and labeled environments. Tail-sampling policy edits and collector drops change which slow traces persist, creating apparent latency shifts without any code change; reconcile otel:collector:log signals with trace volume before declaring regressions.
hardware: Kubernetes-hosted microservices instrumented with OpenTelemetry, exporting traces through OpenTelemetry Collector to Splunk HTTP Event Collector or Observability Cloud, with optional dual indexing on Splunk Enterprise search tiers at 9.2 or newer.
exclusions: Does not replace UC-13.5.2 error-rate-first detection, UC-13.5.3 trace completeness and orphan-span analytics, pure real-user monitoring web vitals without distributed spans, or infrastructure-only saturation alarms that lack trace identifiers. Does not automatically change sampling policies or collector configurations.

controlTest.positiveScenario: In a lab tenant, ingest otel:trace and apm:span events where one checkout service shows a sustained doubling of P99 over prior buckets with adequate span volume; confirm apm_uc_13_5_1_trace_duration_outliers returns page or warn with non-null trace_hotspot_span and inventory enrichment within two scheduled runs.
controlTest.negativeScenario: After recovery when durations return near baseline for at least ninety minutes with healthy span_events counts and quiet collector logs, confirm severities return to info and the alert wrapper emits zero rows when filtering to page and warn only.

evidence: Saved search apm_uc_13_5_1_trace_duration_outliers with scheduled execution, exported results to a restricted observability_evidence index, and dashboard drilldowns tied to APM service views.
